title: Barleriola saturejoides
taxonomy: Barleriola saturejoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Brazil. The plant is classified in the genus Barleriola, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The exact classification of Barleriola saturejoides is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Acanthaceae, genus Barleriola, species B. saturejoides. The classification shows its place in the plant kingdom and its relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Barleriola saturejoides is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the thorny cat's whiskers or Brazilian thorny cat's whiskers. Traditionally, Barleriola saturejoides is used for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of various health issues such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Barleriola saturejoides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
